title: Talks | The Walt Disney Family Museum
text:
# Talks

Learn from animators, Imagineers, and other speakers about Walt Disney's life, career, and legacy.

Members have unlimited access to recordings of Happily Ever After Hours episodes through a special [members-only portal](https://vimeo.com/showcase/7052575) .

Questions about our virtual talks? Visit our [Virtual Programs FAQ here](https://www.waltdisney.org/virtual-experiences/frequently-asked-questions) .

## Upcoming Talks

## Animating Identity: Pixar, Mythology, and the Making of _Mahabharata_ with Sanjay Patel

#### Talks

Sat, May 2 | 1pm

Join us this Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month for an in-depth look at the creative work of animator, writer, illustrator, and director Sanjay Patel. Following a screening of his 2015 short film _Sanjay’s Super Team_ , Patel will discuss his experiences at Pixar Animation Studios...

Read More

[Buy Tickets](https://tickets.wdfmuseum.org/webstore/shop/viewItems.aspx?cg=wdfmws&c=virttalk)

## _The Great Crump Presents His Magic_ with Former Imagineer Chris Crump

#### Talks

Sat, May 30 | 1pm

In celebration of our current special exhibition, _Happiest Place on Earth: The Disneyland Story_ , we are thrilled to welcome former Imagineer Chris Crump for a special presentation of the magical artwork of his father, Disney Legend and Imagineer Rolly Crump.

Read More

[Buy Tickets](https://tickets.wdfmuseum.org/webstore/shop/viewItems.aspx?cg=wdfmws&c=virttalk)

Schedule is subject to change. For the most current schedule of events, please visit our [calendar](https://www.waltdisney.org/calendar) .

### Program Ticketing Policies

For ticketed programs and events, there is a limit of four tickets available for purchase per household, unless otherwise stated. This includes non-paid ticketed programs and events. The museum reserves the right to void tickets purchased in excess of this limit. In some instances, the museum will impose lower limits based on capacity of venue and/or special considerations.

Due to the overwhelming popularity of programs and events at the museum, tickets often sell out quickly. The museum does not collect waitlists for programs or events; however, if tickets to a sold-out program or event become available, they will be released for purchase on the museum’s website at 10am the day before the event.

If you have purchased tickets to a program or event and are no longer able to attend, you may donate your tickets back to the museum for resale. In order to do so, please send an email to tickets@wdfmuseum.org by 10am, one day before the program or event and provide the confirmation number you received when purchasing the tickets within the body of the email. After 10am one day prior to the program or event, we can no longer accept tickets for donation. Tickets for donation cannot be accepted over the phone or in person at the Ticket Desk.

Please note: Children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by an adult who shall be responsible for the child at all times. Tickets reserved for children under the age of 5 without an accompanying adult ticket in the same order will be automatically returned. Lapsitting infants under the age of 1 do not require an additional ticket.

### Accessibility

Guests of The Walt Disney Family Museum can request American Sign Language (ASL) interpreters on-demand for our Talks, Workshops, School Experiences, Tours, and Storytimes. If you would like to request an ASL interpreter, please fill out the form linked below. Please note: Interpreter availability is not guaranteed and requests must be submitted at least two weeks in advance.

The museum is proud to partner with Bay Area Communication Access (BACA) for our ASL interpreter needs. For more information on their services, please visit [BACA's website](https://bacainterp.com/) .

For more information on our Accessibility offerings, please visit our Accessibility page by clicking the link below.

[Accessibility](https://www.waltdisney.org/visit/visitor-information/accessibility)
